methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Long Wall is a seed-stage company in Defense & Security founded by Dan Piemont and Harry O'Hanley. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables spanning seed and Series A, including Tenna Systems ($60M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Stark Defence ($500M), and Scale AI ($29.0B). The broader comparison universe consists of 8 private peers and 2 public companies in the defense technology space.

The peer-median approach is the applicable triangulation method given available data. The five selected comparables yield a median valuation of $300M, with a range extending from $60M to $29.0B. The wide spread reflects heterogeneity in funding stage and business maturity within the Defense & Security sector at early growth phases. This median benchmark directly informs the valuation estimate.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $255M to $345M, centered on $300M. However, this result should be interpreted with material caution. The peer set exhibits substantial variance—Scale AI's $29.0B valuation represents an outlier relative to the seed-stage positioning of Long Wall and the mid-stage clustering around $150M–$500M. The single-method approach limits conviction, and sector-specific factors including technology differentiation, IP defensibility, regulatory environment, and customer concentration remain unquantified. Additional comparable valuation methods would strengthen confidence in any pricing decision.
